summary: Large lbaudio_ax decompilation/matching PR focused on Melee's lb audio AX layer. It replaced many placeholder stubs with C for sound-bank remapping/loading, sound object update callbacks, pan/volume interpolation, background/aux audio setup, and several near-matching functions. The decomp.dev bot reported +1780 matched code bytes, +40 matched data bytes, 6 new matches, and 16 unmatched-item improvements, while also noting regressions in .bss, AXDriver_8038E034 after renaming, fn_80023254, .sdata, and fn_800268B4.

key_files.why: Main decompilation target; added most of the PR's code, including implementations for lbAudioAx_800233EC, lbAudioAx_80023B24, lbAudioAx_800243F4, fn_800253D8, fn_800256BC, fn_80025FAC, fn_800262A0, fn_80026650, fn_800269AC, fn_80026C04, lbAudioAx_80027168, lbAudioAx_80027648, lbAudioAx_8002785C, lbAudioAx_80027AB0, lbAudioAx_80027DF8, lbAudioAx_8002838C, and lbAudioAx_80028690.

Expanded lbAudioAx_UserData and lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc layouts, added/static-adjusted globals such as lbl_804D38D0, sfx_remap_803BC6A4, lbl_804D3870, and lbl_804D3878, and exposed several old array symbols as externs.
Updated public prototypes away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and corrected return types/signatures for several lbAudioAx and fn_80026xxx functions.
Renamed AXDriver_8038E034 to HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize, matching the newly used semantic name for aux effect heap sizing.
Updated the declaration for HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize at address 38E034.
Added baselib/random.h include, likely required after random API header cleanup or dependency exposure.

what_changed: Implemented several previously stubbed lbaudio_ax functions, with decomp.dev reporting full matches for fn_800262A0 and lbAudioAx_800243F4.

Improved multiple large unmatched lbaudio_ax functions, including lbAudioAx_80027DF8, lbAudioAx_80028690, lbAudioAx_80027AB0, lbAudioAx_8002838C, lbAudioAx_80023B24, fn_80025FAC, fn_800253D8, fn_800256BC, lbAudioAx_800233EC, lbAudioAx_8002785C, lbAudioAx_80027168, fn_800269AC, fn_80026C04, lbAudioAx_80027648, fn_80027488, and fn_80026650.
Reworked lbaudio_ax state access from raw global array symbols like lbl_80433B44/lbl_804338A4/lbl_80433984 into fields on lbl_80433710, especially x194, x274, x354, and x434.
Expanded lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc from a small allocator-plus-arrays struct into a large 0x1F554-byte struct containing bank state arrays and aux effect heaps x554 and xD954.

Expanded lbAudioAx_UserData from mostly padding into named fields for owning gobj, entity, callback, sfx id, start/end values, pan fields, current/end frames, direction scalar x3C, group x40, and voice id.
Added a local SoundParams struct in lbaudio_ax.c and used it to initialize lbAudioAx_UserData in fn_80025FAC.
Added audio-related includes to lbaudio_ax.c, including dolphin/ai.h, dolphin/ar.h, dolphin/axfx.h, baselib/debug.h, baselib/random.h, gm_16AE.h, lb_0192.h, and player.h.
Renamed AXDriver_8038E034 to HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize in axdriver.c and axdriver.h.

Changed lbaudio_ax.h prototypes to concrete return types, for example lbAudioAx_80023B24 from f32 to s32, lbAudioAx_800243F4 to s32, fn_80026650 to s32 fn_80026650(void), fn_800269AC to void, fn_80026C04 to s32, lbAudioAx_80027DF8 to void, and lbAudioAx_80028690 to s32.
Introduced or refined pan/volume update logic that clamps values to 0..0x7F before sending doubled/even values to AXDriver_8038D2B4 and AXDriver_8038D3B8.
Added aux effect setup code using AXFX_REVERBSTD, AXFX_DELAY, AXDriver_8038E498, AXDriver_8038E37C, and AXDriver_8038E30C.
The bot report recorded regressions in lbaudio_ax .bss, AXDriver_8038E034/HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize matching, fn_80023254, lbaudio_ax .sdata, and fn_800268B4.
smart_moves: Promoting the global audio pool from loose address arithmetic into lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc fields made repeated accesses like x194/x274/x354/x434 more readable and reusable across functions.

Typing lbAudioAx_UserData unlocked clearer callback and update code: entity classifier access, callback dispatch through x10, frame interpolation fields, pan fields, and voice_id are now represented directly instead of as opaque padding.
The lbAudioAx_800243F4 implementation used an intentional fallthrough switch with repeated increments to reproduce a table-like ID mapping while preserving codegen-sensitive control flow.
The pan/volume update routines explicitly clamp to the AX 0..0x7F domain and then pass `(value * 2) & 0xFE`, documenting the conversion from game-side 7-bit values to AX driver values.

Renaming AXDriver_8038E034 to HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize captured a semantic function name based on its aux effect heap-size role, even though the bot noted it regressed that function's current match percentage.
Moving SoundParams before fn_80025FAC allowed the initializer function to be written with structured field access rather than anonymous stack/parameter offsets.

The PR preserved match-oriented oddities where needed, such as duplicated interpolation blocks and empty `if (ud && ud) {}` blocks in fn_80025B44/fn_80025CBC, which appear codegen-motivated from the diff context.
decomp_lessons: For lbaudio_ax, many globals around lbl_80433710 are probably contiguous fields of one large pool/manager object; prefer testing a struct layout before proliferating separate array globals.

Audio object update callbacks depend strongly on lbAudioAx_UserData layout. Naming fields like current_frame, end_frame, pan_left, pan_right, start_val, end_val, voice_id, entity, and callback clarifies later matching work.
When an address-named baselib function's behavior is understood, a semantic HSD_* name can be introduced, but the resulting match regression should be tracked and revisited.
Fallthrough-heavy switch statements can be a useful matching tactic for sparse audio/SFX ID remap logic where a lookup table would not match original codegen.
Repeated nearly identical interpolation code may be necessary for matching; avoid prematurely factoring helper functions if the original compiler emitted duplicated blocks.

Struct expansion can improve source quality but may destabilize data matching, as shown by the bot-reported .bss and .sdata regressions. Keep data-section changes under review when consolidating globals.
AX driver calls often expect clamped and scaled pan/volume values; reuse the clamp-to-0x7F then `(value * 2) & 0xFE` idiom where evidence supports it.
naming_conventions: Use lbAudioAx_* for known library-audio AX functions and retain address suffixes when semantic names are not known.
Use fn_800xxxxx for internal/static or still-unknown functions where no semantic name is available.
Use HSD_Audio* for semantically identified sysdolphin/baselib audio APIs, as in HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize.

Keep placeholder field names with offset-style identifiers such as x10, x20, x2C, x3C, x40 when field semantics are not fully proven, but name fields with clear behavior such as gobj, entity, start_val, end_val, current_frame, end_frame, pan_left, pan_right, pan, and voice_id.
Use address-suffixed global labels like lbl_80433710, lbl_803BB300, lbl_803BB8D4, and lbl_804D38D0 when full semantic names are not established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used intentional `goto` labels and fallthrough switch comments to preserve codegen-sensitive control flow.
Converted some do/while decrement loops into for-loops where that matched better in fn_80023254, while that function still regressed according to the bot.
Used direct struct field access on lbl_80433710 instead of pointer arithmetic against a char* base for better matching/readability in several functions.
Kept duplicated interpolation implementations in fn_800253D8 and fn_800256BC rather than abstracting them, likely to preserve generated code shape.
Inserted no-op-looking empty `if (ud && ud) {}` blocks in fn_80025B44 and fn_80025CBC, suggesting a stack/register scheduling or branch-shape matching tactic.
Used local scoped blocks and temporary variables around float interpolation and clamp calculations to control expression ordering.
Used typed AXFX_REVERBSTD and AXFX_DELAY stack structs for aux setup rather than opaque buffers.

review_feedback.feedback: Only says "Tracking PR as usual"; no detailed human rationale was provided.
No review comments were present in the provided slice.
Reported 6 new matches and 16 improvements, but also highlighted regressions: lbaudio_ax .bss, AXDriver_8038E034/HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize, fn_80023254, lbaudio_ax .sdata, and fn_800268B4.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs touching lbaudio_ax .bss or .sdata regressions introduced around lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc expansion.
Search for follow-up work on HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize / AXDriver_8038E034 to recover the 53.61% match regression.
Look for later PRs renaming lbAudioAx_UserData fields x10, x20, x3C, x40, and x44 to semantic names.
Find later lbaudio_ax PRs resolving the TODO comment "This is 100% permute-able" in fn_80025E38.
Search for later matching of lbAudioAx_80023B24 and lbAudioAx_800233EC, which this PR improved but did not fully match.
